Remarks: Family of 5 Northern Rufous Hornbills where encountered in the field. Flock was foraging on mid and upper canopy of fruiting trees in secondary forest. No playback was used. No filters applied. Adults sex were distinguished by eye color (pale blue & yellow-orange). Sub-adults had nearly adult plumage but blackish-red bills. Juvenile had creamy brown plumage and all black bill.

Remarks: Recorded overnight - farmland/riverine habitat. Some kind of Rail?

I recorded this bird song/call on my phone during 7:47 AM. I have been trying to find what type of bird this is, since I've always heard this song/call often. Something I did notice is that the bird often sings it during early mornings, and sometimes during sundown, around 5:00-6:00 PM. Unfortunately, I do not hear it daily, especially when rainy days happen. In person, this bird song/call was generally loud that it was super noticeable. The place where I recorded this audio had a few trees around such as pili trees, mango trees, etc.
